title: RC Underwater ROV — Thruster Array and Waterproof Electronics
design_question: How to build a remotely controlled underwater exploration drone with camera and manoeuvring capability?
selected_approach: 6-thruster ROV: 4 horizontal (vectored ±45° for surge/sway/yaw) + 2 vertical (heave). BlueRobotics T200 thrusters (2.9 kgf, 12–20 V). Electronics in IP68 aluminium tube (100 mm dia, O-ring end-caps). Raspberry Pi Zero W for H.264 video streaming via WiFi surface tether (Cat6 Ethernet + 100BASE-T). ArduSub firmware on Pixhawk controls thrusters via 6× BasicESC. Depth sensor (MS5837-30BA, 300 m rated). Tether: 30 m slim neutrally buoyant tether (4 power + 2 data).
rationale: ArduSub provides stable depth hold, heading hold, and stabilisation. Ethernet tether gives low-latency 1080p video. T200 thrusters have known thrust curves for precise control allocation. IP68 tube is repressable and inspectable.
verification_notes: FACT: 'Underwater Thruster — Hydrodynamic Thrust and Propulsion' gives T = ρ·A·(v_j² − v_i²)/2 and T200 spec 2.9 kgf.
supporting_fact_titles: Underwater Thruster — Hydrodynamic Thrust and Propulsion
